hotspots.title: Meloni’s labor decree and Minetti pardon scandal
hotspots.blurb: Italy's government faces intense scrutiny over a controversial pardon for Nicole Minetti, linked to former PM Berlusconi, and a new labor decree allocating nearly €1 billion for employment incentives. The pardon has sparked investigations into alleged falsified clemency requests, while the labor decree ties incentives to 'fair wages,' drawing both praise and criticism.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral outrage over the Minetti pardon, perceived as a miscarriage of justice, and the labor decree's polarizing economic implications, framing it as a battle between social justice and government overreach.

hotspots.title: Trump’s WHCD evacuation after shooting
hotspots.blurb: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner led to the evacuation of President Trump and attendees. The suspect, armed with guns and knives, was charged with attempting to assassinate Trump. The event has reignited debates over security protocols and political violence in the U.S.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the visceral fear of political assassination, the spectacle of a high-profile security breach, and the polarized framing of the incident as either a security failure or a politically motivated attack.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain's conservative PP and far-right Vox have formed regional pacts in Aragón and Extremadura, prioritizing 'national citizens' in public services and social aid. The agreements have sparked nationwide debates over immigration, healthcare access, and constitutional legality, with left-wing parties accusing the coalition of fostering 'apartheid.'
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral and legal controversy over 'national priority' policies, the polarization between left and right over immigration, and the high-stakes regional governance implications that could reshape Spain's political landscape.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany's far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) has surged in polls, leading the conservative Union by five points. The party's growing influence has sparked debates over constitutional loyalty, cooperation with mainstream parties, and potential bans. Meanwhile, protests and institutional pushback highlight the democratic backlash against the AfD's extremist rhetoric.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the existential threat AfD's rise poses to Germany's democratic norms, the alarmist framing of its extremist ties, and the polarized contention over whether to engage or isolate the party.

hotspots.title: Tisza’s victory and Fidesz’s post-Orbán reshuffle
hotspots.blurb: Hungary's opposition Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, won a landslide victory in the April elections, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule. The new government has pledged to release secret police files, investigate corruption, and reset EU relations. Meanwhile, Fidesz faces internal reshuffling, with Orbán offering to step down as party leader but remaining influential.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the historic shift in Hungarian politics, the moral charge of corruption investigations, and the polarized contention over Orbán's legacy and the new government's reformist agenda.
